title: Evangelion Chronicle
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion Chronicle             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Sun Apr 13, 2008 1:06 pm Does anybody know where I can find a scan of the Eva Chronicle?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Apr 13, 2008 1:12 pm Probably still on Reichu's HD, like last time http://evageeks.org/forum/viewtopic.php?p=160937#160937 PM/Mail her directly.

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Sun Apr 13, 2008 2:37 pm Is there any, on the internet?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Apr 13, 2008 3:11 pm No idea. You can google as well as the rest of us; better, since you can read Japanese.

[]:             Quote Post by Zeak » Thu Apr 24, 2008 3:03 am kinokuniya bookstore is your best bet thats where Im getting them from

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Thu Apr 24, 2008 11:24 am No Kinokuniya's in Britain....

[]:             Quote Post by Zeak » Thu Apr 24, 2008 1:19 pm internet you can order from them i believe

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Apr 26, 2008 8:26 pm I started scanning them in full, but never finished. Processing giant scans takes forever on my computer, and it's just not very fun at all. Won't be able to scan it all, anyway, without Vol. 30. :(

[]:             Quote Post by Zeak » Sat Apr 26, 2008 8:28 pm im working on it

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Apr 26, 2008 8:32 pm Would you be able to secure a copy for me? I tried to get one through my usual source (Akadot), but they couldn't get it... Just so there's no confusion, by Vol. 30, I mean this one with Yui-sama on the cover .

[]:             Quote Post by Zeak » Sat Apr 26, 2008 8:34 pm yeah ok when it comes in ill get 2 then

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Jan 01, 2015 8:54 pm Bumping because...Twice today I mentioned the book (once about NGE timeline, and also how both EoTV and EoE are included in the timeline). I get that Sides A and B aren't out in English (yet..March is Side A's release date?) but even with just the Japanese and French versions at our disposal, I'm wondering why don't we have all the resources like relationship charts, series timeline, etc, long available already?

[]:             Quote Post by xPearse » Fri Jan 02, 2015 8:49 am The Imperialist wrote: Is there any, on the internet?

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Fri Jan 02, 2015 10:01 am Would be nice

[]:             Quote Post by vito » Fri Jan 02, 2015 12:16 pm Sure~

[]:             Quote Post by xPearse » Fri Jan 02, 2015 8:28 pm Silly me, I forgot my mega password but I still have them on my hard drive, just forgot to post them on my blog. Anyway, the Russians still have them so here's the link to there page on them with DL's, have fun.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Jan 02, 2015 10:56 pm Link removed. Please take this to PMs. Chronicle's being released in the States. teh Rules

[]:             Quote Post by xPearse » Sat Jan 03, 2015 8:15 am Maybe the art books/illustrations, but not the actual 2006 magazine series. Or is it?

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sat Jan 03, 2015 12:16 pm I think Udon is doing the illustrations and Side A/B.

[]:             Quote Post by xPearse » Sat Jan 03, 2015 8:08 pm Ornette wrote: I think Udon is doing the illustrations and Side A/B.
summary:

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Apr 13, 2008 1:12 pm Probably still on Reichu's HD, like last time http://evageeks.org/forum/viewtopic.php?p=160937#160937 PM/Mail her directly.
